title: Why CRM Projects Fail Before Development Begins
summary: Unclear ownership, unstated assumptions and premature software decisions do most of the damage long before anyone opens a build environment.

title: Standard Functionality or Custom Build?
summary: A decision framework for choosing the simplest implementable option without quietly weakening the business process.

title: Why Technology Should Come After Process Understanding
summary: Choosing applications too early is how businesses end up with five systems and one spreadsheet that still runs everything.

title: How Hidden Scope Enters Software Projects
summary: The routine ways assumptions, dependencies and client responsibilities go unrecorded — and what it costs when they surface in UAT.

title: Two Systems, One Record: Deciding the Source of Truth
summary: When a CRM sits over an ERP, most of the argument is about which system owns the customer — and answering it late is expensive.

title: Discovery Questions That Actually Surface the Truth
summary: Why 'show me the last five real records' beats 'walk me through your process', and other questions that get past the official version.

title: Designing for the Person Who Did Not Ask for This
summary: Adoption is a design constraint, not a training problem. What changes when you design for reluctance.

title: Defect or Change Request? The Line That Decides the Project
summary: Most scope disputes are one unwritten definition away from never happening.

title: Using AI as a Consulting Intelligence Layer
summary: Transcript analysis, requirement structuring, gap auditing and reusable knowledge — leverage over judgement, not instead of it.

title: Writing Client-Facing Documents Under Someone Else's Brand
summary: What has to come out of a document before it reaches the end client — and why internal reasoning is not evidence of rigour.
